Title: Mechanistic basis for the emergence of EPS1 as a catalyst in salicylic acid biosynthesis of Brassicaceae

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in yeast for pathway reconstruction
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen attack discussed as inducing primary SA route in Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and related physiological roles
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies isochorismate precursor required for isochorismate-derived SA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SID2 converts chorismate to isochorismate in plastids. 2. Coexpression with PBS3 in yeast was sufficient to produce SA. 3. Addition of EPS1 further enhanced flux from IGA to SA/NPG.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous coexpression in yeast with metabolite profiling; prior pathway knowledge referenced
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics by LC-HRAM-MS; no accession reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be combined with downstream SA-pathway genes for SA engineering
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although plastid-localized in plants, yeast reconstitution did not require compartmentation

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Functions in SA biosynthesis downstream of isochorismate export
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in yeast for pathway reconstruction
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and related physiological roles
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms the unstable intermediate IGA from isochorismate and L-glutamate, thereby enabling SA formation; with SID2 alone can support some SA formation in yeast
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PBS3 catalyzes regiospecific conjugation of isochorismate with L-glutamate to form IGA. 2. Coexpression of PBS3 with SID2 in yeast generated SA even without SID1. 3. EPS1 markedly increased conversion efficiency beyond the spontaneous decay of IGA.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous coexpression in yeast with metabolite profiling; prior pathway knowledge referenced
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics by LC-HRAM-MS; no accession reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with SID2/EPS1 to engineer SA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: IGA spontaneously decays to SA and NPG, but much more slowly than with EPS1

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Associated with isochorismate export during SA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not required in yeast pathway reconstruction
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and related physiological roles
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transports isochorismate from plastid to cytosol in Arabidopsis; dispensable in cytosolic yeast reconstitution
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SID1 is the plastid-envelope transporter for isochorismate in Arabidopsis. 2. The yeast reconstruction bypassed the need for SID1 because the pathway was reconstituted in the cytosol.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Contextual pathway reconstruction inference in yeast
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant when engineering compartmentalized SA pathways in plants but unnecessary in non-compartmental heterologous systems

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Glycine max
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: BY4743 (yeast); soybean transgenic lines T1; Arabidopsis accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: p423TEF/p425TEF/p426TEF constitutive expression in yeast; CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in soybean
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal step of the isochorismate/PBS3 branch in Brassicaceae
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; noncanonical HXXXD-family enzyme with Ser substitution in catalytic motif
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced during pathogen-triggered SA biosynthesis; transcript details not otherwise specified in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenic expression in yeast cytosol and soybean leaves; higher transgene expression in severe soybean phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen attack/Pseudomonas syringae discussed for Arabidopsis SA induction; greenhouse long-day growth for soybean
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Accelerates decomposition of isochorismoyl-glutamate A to salicylic acid and N-pyruvoyl-L-glutamate via a pericyclic rearrangement lyase mechanism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling, local and systemic pathogen responses; also roles in photosynthesis, ion uptake/transport, and growth regulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves in soybean; generally plant tissues undergoing defense responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes/accelerates salicylic acid accumulation from IGA; transgenic expression in soybean elevates basal SA and can cause stunted growth and leaf yellowing at high expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EPS1 is a Brassicaceae-specific BAHD-family enzyme that acquired isochorismoyl-glutamate pyruvoyl-glutamate lyase activity. 2. Structural, MD, and QM analyses support a pericyclic elimination mechanism that lowers the reaction barrier relative to the non-enzymatic reaction. 3. In yeast coexpressing SID2 and PBS3, AtEPS1 increased SA about sevenfold and NPG about sixfold while depleting IGA by more than tenfold. 4. Stable expression of AtEPS1 in soybean, which lacks an EPS1 ortholog, increased SA and caused SA-overaccumulation-associated growth defects in high-expressing lines.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: X-ray crystallography; molecular dynamics and QM modeling; heterologous pathway reconstitution in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; site-directed mutagenesis; LC-HRAM-MS metabolite profiling; stable transgenic overexpression in soybean; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics by LC-HRAM-MS; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant/accelerating enzyme for the last step of Brassicaceae isochorismate-derived SA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High ectopic expression in soybean caused SA overaccumulation, stunted growth, and leaf yellowing
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering enhanced SA-mediated disease resistance in non-Brassicaceae crops, but expression strength must be controlled to avoid growth penalties
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Soybean lacks an EPS1 ortholog; active-site residues Thr306 and Arg395 appear selected in the EPS1 clade for IPGL activity; PDB structures reported as 6WCS and 6WAO
